docs.title: HARMONIX
docs.text:
A harmonization pipeline framework for polysomnography data

HARMONIX is an open-source framework for harmonizing polysomnographic (PSG) data for public distribution via the National Sleep Research Resource (NSRR). It is built on top of Luna and designed to compress a week-long harmonization process into a single day.

HARMONIX is developed at Brigham and Women's Hospital as part of the NAPS Consortium harmonization effort.

docs.title: What HARMONIX does
docs.text:
Harmonizing a PSG dataset for public release involves many steps: checking EDF files, removing PHI, mapping channel names, assessing signal quality, reviewing staging, documenting issues, and generating a distribution-ready dataset with full documentation. Done manually, this takes 5–7 days per dataset.

HARMONIX automates and accelerates every step of this process — with built-in fallback logic that flags unusual cases for human review rather than silently passing them.

docs.title: Core design principles
docs.text:
Automation with human-in-the-loop fallback. Every automated check produces one of three outcomes: pass, soft flag (unusual — review later), or hard flag (pipeline pauses — review now). Nothing unusual is silently ignored.

Documentation is generated, not written. The README follows the APPLES/NSRR standard format. Every tool writes its outputs directly into the README as it runs. By the time processing finishes, the documentation is already done.

Built on Luna. HARMONIX is a framework around Luna, not a replacement for it. All signal processing uses Luna commands directly.

docs.title: Pipeline overview
docs.text:
Stage Tool Time 00 Project Scaffolder 5 min 01 PHI + File Naming Validator 15 min 02 AI-assisted cmap Generator 30 min 03 SLURM Parallel QC Pipeline 2–3 hrs 04 Signal Coverage Dashboard 1–2 hrs review 05 Manual Review Queue (LunaScope) 2–3 hrs 06 Auto Issues File Generator 1 hr review 07 README Report Finalizer Auto

See the Architecture page for the full pipeline diagram and fallback logic.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/your-github-username/harmonix
cd harmonix
pip install -e .

Then follow the Getting Started guide, or jump straight to the Your First Dataset tutorial.

docs.title: Things HARMONIX aims to do
docs.text:
• Scaffold standardized project directories for any new dataset
  • Validate EDF headers for PHI and file naming compliance
  • Generate Luna cmap.txt channel mappings with AI-assisted suggestions
  • Run parallelized signal QC across all subjects on SLURM clusters
  • Produce a signal coverage dashboard stratified by domain and study type
  • Auto-generate a structured issues file from QC outputs
  • Compile a fully-populated README report in NSRR standard format

docs.title: Things HARMONIX doesn't aim to do
docs.text:
• Replace Luna — all signal processing is delegated to Luna
  • Perform clinical scoring or event detection
  • Replace manual expert review — the goal is to minimize it, not eliminate it
  • Support non-EDF data formats

Status

HARMONIX is under active development. Tools are being built and documented incrementally. See the Tools section for current status of each component.

docs.title: Pipeline Architecture
docs.text: HARMONIX compresses a 5–7 day PSG harmonization process into a single day by automating discovery and front-loading all QC checks so that human time is spent only on resolving problems, not finding them.

docs.title: Design philosophy
docs.text:
The key insight is that most of the manual time in harmonization is spent discovering problems — opening files, scrolling through LunaScope, noticing things look wrong. HARMONIX automates discovery entirely. You spend human time only on the subset of cases that are genuinely unusual.

Three principles drive the design:

  1. Automation with fallback — every check produces pass / soft flag / hard flag
  2. README as living document — outputs are written into the README as the pipeline runs
  3. Luna-native — all signal processing delegates to Luna commands directly

docs.title: Fallback tiers
docs.text:
Every automated check in HARMONIX produces one of three outcomes:

Tier Meaning Action ✅ Pass Within expected range Write to README, continue 🟡 Soft flag Unusual but not blocking Add to review queue, continue 🔴 Hard flag Cannot proceed without human decision Pause subject, add to review queue

The review queue (res/review_queue.txt) is your prioritized checklist at the end of the automated run. Hard flags first, soft flags second.

docs.title: Stage 00 — Project Scaffolder
docs.text:
Time: ~5 min | Mode: Auto

Creates the standard NSRR folder structure and initializes a README pre-filled with sections 0–13 (matching the APPLES/NSRR standard).

harmonix scaffold --dataset mydata --path /data/nsrr/working/mydata

docs.title: Stage 01 — PHI + File Naming Validator
docs.text:
Time: ~15 min | Mode: Auto

Scans all EDF headers for PHI (names, dates, MRNs). Validates file naming conventions. Checks for duplicates and unexpected EDF types.

Luna commands used: luna --build, luna --validate, HEADERS, DESC, ANON

Soft flags: unexpected date format, non-standard filename Hard flags: real name in header, duplicate subject IDs

docs.title: Stage 02 — AI-assisted cmap Generator
docs.text:
Time: ~30 min | Mode: AI + Human review

Extracts channel inventory from all EDFs. Compares against a reference alias library built from NAPS, CHAT, nuMoM2b, and LOFT datasets. An LLM suggests canonical mappings with confidence scores. You review only the uncertain ones.

Luna commands used: HEADERS signals, CANONICAL, ALIASES

Output: ready-to-use cmap.txt, README §7 populated

Soft flags: low-confidence mappings queued for review

docs.title: Stage 03 — SLURM Parallel QC Pipeline
docs.text:
Time: 2–3 hrs (parallel) | Mode: SLURM

The most compute-intensive stage. Runs full signal quality checks across all subjects and channels in parallel on ERISTwo. Produces per-subject, per-epoch QC metrics for every domain.

Luna commands used: SIGSTATS, STATS, SOAP, HYPNO, HRV, FILTER, PEAKS, POL

Soft flags: amplitude >3×IQR, SOAP kappa <0.4, sleep efficiency <50% Hard flags: >40% epochs flat, staging/EDF duration mismatch, zero-variance channel

docs.title: Stage 04 — Signal Coverage Dashboard
docs.text:
Time: 1–2 hrs review | Mode: Auto + Human review

Extends the channel inventory tool. For each domain (EEG, ECG, respiratory, SpO2, EMG) shows % subjects with signal present and % passing QC, broken down by study type (diagnostic vs CPAP vs split-night). Auto-generates inclusion/exclusion rationale at configurable thresholds (default: present in >80% of subjects).

Soft flags: site-level outliers in amplitude distributions

docs.title: Stage 05 — Targeted LunaScope Inspection
docs.text:
Time: 2–3 hrs | Mode: Manual

Only flagged items from the review queue are opened in LunaScope. Instead of inspecting all subjects, you inspect only those with hard or soft flags. Hard flags reviewed first.

docs.title: Stage 06 — Auto Issues File Generator
docs.text:
Time: 1 hr review | Mode: Auto + Human review

Every QC check that fails writes a structured entry to the issues file automatically. Categories: signal quality, PHI/headers, file naming, annotation issues, unit errors, channel absence. You add narrative context only where needed.

docs.title: Stage 07 — README Report Finalizer
docs.text:
Time: Auto | Mode: Auto

Compiles the living README (populated throughout the pipeline) into a clean HTML report. Mirrors the NSRR APPLES README structure exactly.

harmonix report --readme README.md --out dist/report.html

docs.title: Full pipeline flow
docs.text:
New Dataset Arrives
        │
        ▼
[00] Project Scaffolder          ← 5 min
        │
        ▼
[01] PHI + File Validator         ← 15 min
        │
   ┌────┴────┐
 HARD       SOFT
 pause     queue
        │
        ▼
[02] AI cmap Generator            ← 30 min
        │
        ▼
[03] SLURM QC Pipeline            ← 2–3 hrs parallel
        │
   ┌────┴────┐
 HARD       SOFT
 pause     queue
        │
        ▼
[04] Signal Coverage Dashboard    ← auto + 1–2 hrs review
        │
        ▼
[05] Manual Review Queue          ← 2–3 hrs (flagged only)
        │
        ▼
[06] Issues File Generator        ← auto + 1 hr review
        │
        ▼
[07] README Report Finalizer      ← auto
        │
        ▼
Harmonized Dataset + README Report

Total: ~1 working day

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before using HARMONIX, you need:

  • Luna v1.2+ installed and accessible via command line. See Luna installation.
  • Python 3.9+
  • R 4.0+ (for coverage dashboard and report generation)
  • Access to an SLURM cluster (ERISTwo or equivalent) for the QC pipeline
  • A Luna sample list for the dataset you want to harmonize

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Clone the repo and install:

git clone https://github.com/TejasKarkera10/harmonix
cd harmonix
pip install -e .

Install MkDocs if you want to build the docs locally:

pip install mkdocs-material

docs.title: 1. Scaffold a new project
docs.text:
harmonix scaffold --dataset apples --path /data/nsrr/working/apples

This creates the standard folder structure:

apples/
├── files/       # original non-data files (PDFs, XLS, etc.)
├── sl/          # sample lists
├── tmp/         # raw Luna outputs (.db files)
├── res/         # compiled higher-level outputs
├── cmd/         # Luna scripts
├── nap/         # NAP output and harmonized EDFs
├── dist/        # final distribution folder
└── viz/         # visual summaries (HTML)

And initializes a README.md pre-filled with the NSRR standard sections (0–13).

docs.title: 2. Run PHI validation
docs.text: harmonix phi --sl sl/s0.lst --out res/phi_report.txt

docs.title: 3. Generate cmap
docs.text: harmonix cmap --sl sl/s0.lst --ref reference/aliases.txt --out cmd/cmap.txt

docs.title: 4. Run QC pipeline
docs.text: harmonix qc --sl sl/s0.lst --cmd cmd/ --out tmp/ --jobs 20

docs.title: Sample lists
docs.text:
HARMONIX uses Luna sample lists (.lst files) as its primary input format. Build one with:

luna --build /path/to/edfs/ > sl/s0.lst

docs.title: The review queue
docs.text:
Every automated check writes flagged items to res/review_queue.txt:

[HARD] id_140003 — staging duration 6h12m vs EDF 8h45m
[SOFT] id_350094 — recording duration 11.2h, dataset median 7.8h

Hard flags pause processing for that subject. Soft flags continue but are queued for later review.

docs.title: README as living document
docs.text: The README is not written at the end — it is populated throughout the pipeline. Every tool appends its outputs directly into the relevant README section. By the time you finish, documentation is already done.

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text:
• Read the Architecture page for the full pipeline design
  • Browse the Tools reference
  • Follow the Your First Dataset tutorial end-to-end

docs.title: Tools
docs.text: HARMONIX is composed of seven tools, each targeting a specific bottleneck in the harmonization process. Each tool can be run independently or as part of the full pipeline.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Tool Status CLI Command Project Scaffolder 🚧 In development harmonix scaffold PHI Validator 🚧 In development harmonix phi cmap Generator 🚧 In development harmonix cmap QC Pipeline 🚧 In development harmonix qc Coverage Dashboard 🚧 In development harmonix coverage Issues Generator 🚧 In development harmonix issues Report Finalizer 🚧 In development harmonix report

docs.title: Running the full pipeline
docs.text:
harmonix run --dataset apples --path /data/nsrr/working/apples --jobs 20

This runs all stages sequentially, pausing on hard flags for manual resolution before continuing.

docs.title: Running individual tools
docs.text:
Each tool can be run standalone:

harmonix scaffold --dataset apples --path /data/nsrr/working/apples
harmonix phi --sl sl/s0.lst
harmonix cmap --sl sl/s0.lst --ref reference/aliases.txt
harmonix qc --sl sl/s0.lst --jobs 20
harmonix coverage --res res/
harmonix issues --res res/
harmonix report --readme README.md

docs.title: cmap Generator
docs.text: Stage: 02 | Mode: AI-assisted + Human review | Time: ~30 min

docs.title: What it does
docs.text:
Extracts the full channel inventory from all EDFs in the dataset, compares against a reference alias library built from NAPS, CHAT, nuMoM2b, and LOFT datasets, and uses an LLM to suggest canonical Luna channel mappings. You review only the uncertain ones.

Output is a ready-to-use Luna cmap.txt file and a populated README §7.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
harmonix cmap --sl <sample_list> [options]

Example: 

harmonix cmap --sl sl/s0.lst --ref reference/aliases.txt --out cmd/cmap.txt

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Required Description --sl Yes Path to Luna sample list --ref No Path to reference alias library (default: built-in NAPS/CHAT/nuMoM2b/LOFT library) --out No Output cmap path (default: cmd/cmap.txt) --confidence No Confidence threshold below which mappings are flagged for review (default: 0.85) --readme No README path to auto-populate §7 (default: README.md)

docs.title: Step 1 — Extract channel inventory
docs.text:
Runs HEADERS signals across all subjects and compiles a frequency table of channel names:

ECG I          1105/1105   (100%)
C3-M2           982/1105   (89%)
THOR RES        801/1105   (73%)
Flow            655/1105   (59%)
...

docs.title: Step 2 — Match against reference library
docs.text: Compares each channel name against the built-in alias library. Channels with exact or near-exact matches are auto-mapped with high confidence.

docs.title: Step 3 — LLM suggestions for uncertain mappings
docs.text: Channel names that don't match the reference library are passed to an LLM with context about the dataset (PSG system, site, domain) to suggest canonical mappings. Each suggestion comes with a confidence score and reasoning.

docs.title: Step 4 — Human review of low-confidence mappings
docs.text: Only mappings below the confidence threshold are presented for human confirmation. Everything else is auto-accepted.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
cmd/cmap.txt — Luna-format channel alias file:

alias  ECG   ECG1   "ECG I"   "ECGI"   "EKG"
alias  THOR  Thor   "THOR RES"   "THORAX"
alias  C3    C3M2   "C3-M2"   "C3:M2"

res/channel_inventory.txt — full channel frequency table

res/review_queue.txt — low-confidence mappings flagged with category [CMAP]

README.md §7 — populated with mapping table

docs.title: Luna commands used
docs.text:
luna sl/s0.lst -o tmp/headers.db -s 'HEADERS signals'
destrat tmp/headers.db +HEADERS -r CH > res/channel_inventory.txt

docs.title: Reference alias library
docs.text:
The built-in library covers channel aliases across:

  • NAPS Consortium (Stanford, UCSF, Mayo, Emory, JHU, BWH)
  • CHAT (pediatric adenotonsillectomy trial)
  • nuMoM2b (nulliparous pregnancy outcomes)
  • LOFT

Domains covered: EEG, EOG, EMG, ECG, respiratory (RIP, flow, thermistor), oximetry, audio, PAP/CPAP, position.

Note

You can extend the reference library by adding entries to reference/aliases.txt in your local HARMONIX installation.

docs.title: Coverage Dashboard
docs.text: Stage: 04 | Mode: Auto + Human review | Time: 1–2 hrs review

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Extends the channel inventory with QC pass rates. For each signal domain, shows what percentage of subjects have the signal present and what percentage pass QC — broken down by study type and site. Auto-generates inclusion/exclusion rationale at configurable thresholds.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
harmonix coverage --res <results_dir> [options]

Example: 

harmonix coverage --res res/ --threshold 0.8 --out res/coverage_report.html

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Required Description --res Yes Path to results directory (from QC pipeline) --threshold No Minimum proportion present to include signal (default: 0.8) --out No Output HTML dashboard (default: res/coverage_report.html) --readme No README path to auto-populate §4 (default: README.md)

docs.title: Output table structure
docs.text: Domain Channel Present (%) QC Pass (%) Diagnostic CPAP Split-night Decision EEG C3 98% 94% 99% 97% 91% ✅ Include ECG ECG1 100% 96% 100% 100% 100% ✅ Include Resp THOR 87% 71% 92% 85% 74% ✅ Include Resp ABDO 84% 68% 90% 82% 71% ✅ Include Resp Flow 61% 55% 65% 58% 52% 🟡 Review

docs.title: Soft flags
docs.text:
• Any channel present in <80% of subjects for a specific site (site-level outlier)
  • QC pass rate <60% for any included channel
  • Large discrepancy in channel availability between study types

docs.title: Issues File Generator
docs.text: Stage: 06 | Mode: Auto + Human review | Time: ~1 hr review

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Compiles all flagged items from the review queue into a structured issues file — categorized by type, severity, and subject. Every QC failure is auto-written as a structured entry. You add narrative context only where needed.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
harmonix issues --res <results_dir> [options]

Example: 

harmonix issues --res res/ --out res/issues.md

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Required Description --res Yes Path to results directory --out No Output issues file (default: res/issues.md) --readme No README to update (default: README.md)

docs.title: Issue categories
docs.text: Category Tag Examples Signal quality [SQ] Flatline, clipping, amplitude outlier PHI / headers [PHI] Name in header, real date File naming [FILE] Non-standard naming, duplicate ID Annotation [ANNOT] Stage gaps, duration mismatch Unit errors [UNIT] mV vs uV mislabel Channel absence [CH] Expected channel missing from site

docs.title: Output format
docs.text:
## Issues — APPLES dataset
_Generated 2026-03-05 by harmonix v0.1_

### Hard flags (require resolution before distribution)

**[PHI] id_140003** — Real subject name found in EDF Patient Name field.
Resolved: stripped with `ANON`, new EDF written.

**[ANNOT] id_220011** — Staging duration 6h12m vs EDF duration 8h45m.
Status: under review.

### Soft flags (documented, not blocking)

**[SQ] id_350094** — Recording duration 11.2h (dataset median 7.8h).
Note: confirmed valid long recording from site SU.

**[SQ] id_180022** — SOAP kappa 0.38 for C3-M2, typical for C4-M1.
Note: C3 electrode likely suboptimal; C4 retained as primary EEG.

docs.title: PHI + File Naming Validator
docs.text: Stage: 01 | Mode: Auto | Time: ~15 min

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Scans all EDF headers across the dataset for protected health information (PHI) and validates file naming conventions. Outputs a structured report and populates README §3 (Identifiers).

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
harmonix phi --sl <sample_list> [options]

Example: 

harmonix phi --sl sl/s0.lst --out res/phi_report.txt

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Required Description --sl Yes Path to Luna sample list --out No Output report path (default: res/phi_report.txt) --readme No README path to auto-populate §3 (default: README.md)

docs.title: PHI checks (EDF header fields)
docs.text: Field Check Patient ID Must be numeric or anonymized format Patient name Must be empty or anonymized Start date Must be anonymized (01.01.85 standard) Birthdate Must be empty or anonymized Recording field Must not contain technician name or site name

docs.title: File naming checks
docs.text: Check Description Consistent prefix All files should follow the same naming pattern No PHI in filename Filenames must not contain dates, names, or MRNs Unique IDs No duplicate subject IDs across the sample list EDF type consistency All files expected to be same type (EDF vs EDF+)

docs.title: Fallback logic
docs.text: Outcome Trigger Action ✅ Pass All checks clear Write summary to README §3, continue 🟡 Soft flag Unexpected date format, non-standard filename Add to review_queue.txt, continue 🔴 Hard flag Real name in header, duplicate IDs Add to review_queue.txt, pause subject

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
res/phi_report.txt — per-subject pass/fail for each check

res/review_queue.txt — flagged items appended with category [PHI]

README.md §3 — populated with summary counts and any issues found

docs.title: Luna commands used
docs.text:
luna sl/s0.lst -s 'HEADERS'
luna sl/s0.lst -s 'DESC'

docs.title: QC Pipeline
docs.text: Stage: 03 | Mode: SLURM parallel | Time: 2–3 hrs

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: The most compute-intensive stage. Runs full signal quality checks across all subjects and channels simultaneously using SLURM array jobs on ERISTwo. Produces per-subject, per-epoch QC metrics for every signal domain, stratified by sleep stage.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
harmonix qc --sl <sample_list> [options]

Example: 

harmonix qc --sl sl/s0.lst --cmap cmd/cmap.txt --out tmp/ --jobs 20

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Required Description --sl Yes Path to Luna sample list --cmap No Path to channel alias file (default: cmd/cmap.txt) --out No Output directory for .db files (default: tmp/) --jobs No Number of SLURM array jobs (default: 20) --partition No SLURM partition (default: short) --readme No README path to auto-populate §4 (default: README.md)

docs.title: Signal-level checks (all channels)
docs.text: Check Method Soft flag Hard flag Flatline detection Hjorth activity (SIGSTATS) >20% epochs flat >40% epochs flat Amplitude range STATS vs dataset IQR >3× IQR >10× IQR Clipping SIGSTATS clip detection Any clipping >10% epochs clipped Sample rate HEADERS vs expected — Unexpected SR Unit labels HEADERS Mismatch warning —

docs.title: ECG-specific checks
docs.text: Check Method Soft flag Hard flag Polarity HRV P_INV P_INV 0.3–0.7 — R-peak quality HRV NP_TOT Low R-peak count Zero R-peaks HR plausibility HRV HR HR <40 or >100 HR <20 or >150

docs.title: Staging checks
docs.text: Check Method Soft flag Hard flag EEG-stage consistency SOAP kappa kappa <0.4 kappa <0.2 Sleep efficiency HYPNO SE <50% or >99% SE = 0% Duration match HEADERS vs staging >10 min mismatch >30 min mismatch Stage coverage HYPNO Gaps in staging No staging

docs.title: Sleep stage stratification
docs.text:
QC metrics are computed both overall and stratified by sleep stage. This matters because:

  • Wake epochs have expected movement artifact — flatline during wake is not a signal failure
  • REM breathing is naturally irregular — strict morphology thresholds would false-positive
  • NREM (N2/N3) is the most stable — best for assessing true signal quality

docs.title: SLURM job structure
docs.text:
The pipeline splits the sample list across N array jobs:

# auto-generated by harmonix qc
sbatch --array=1-20 cmd/run_qc.sh

Each job processes ceil(N_subjects / N_jobs) subjects and writes a .db file to tmp/. Results are merged after all jobs complete.

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
tmp/qc_*.db — per-job Luna output databases

res/qc_summary.txt — compiled per-subject QC metrics

res/review_queue.txt — flagged subjects appended with category [QC]

README.md §4 — populated with signal summary statistics and distribution plots

docs.title: Luna commands used
docs.text:
-- cmd/qc.lsl
EPOCH len=30
HYPNO
SIGSTATS
STATS
HRV sig=${ECG}
SOAP

docs.title: Report Finalizer
docs.text: Stage: 07 | Mode: Auto | Time: ~auto

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Compiles the living README (populated throughout the pipeline) into a clean, shareable HTML report. Mirrors the NSRR APPLES README structure exactly. No writing required — everything was populated as the pipeline ran.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
harmonix report --readme <readme_path> [options]

Example: 

harmonix report --readme README.md --out dist/report.html

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Required Description --readme Yes Path to populated README.md --out No Output HTML path (default: dist/report.html) --css No Custom CSS (default: NSRR github-markdown style)

docs.title: What the report contains
docs.text:
The report is a direct render of the README, which by this point contains:

Section Populated by §0 Preliminaries Scaffolder §1 Key design factors You (manual) §2 EDF checks PHI Validator §3 Identifiers PHI Validator §4 Signal review QC Pipeline + Coverage Dashboard §5 Annotation review QC Pipeline §6 Manual staging You (manual, if applicable) §7 NAP signal mappings cmap Generator §8 NAP annotation mappings You (manual) §9 Encodings Auto §10 Interim report Issues Generator §11 Running NAP Auto §12 Distribution dataset Auto §13 As-is dataset Auto

docs.title: Under the hood
docs.text:
Uses pandoc to convert README.md to standalone HTML:

pandoc --standalone -c github-markdown.css \
  -f gfm -t html \
  -o dist/report.html \
  --metadata title="DATASET|NSRR" \
  README.md

The README IS the report

The core design principle of HARMONIX is that documentation is generated, not written. Every tool writes into the README as it runs. By the time you call harmonix report, the only sections requiring manual content are §1 (study design) and §8 (annotation mappings) — everything else is already populated.

docs.title: Project Scaffolder
docs.text: Stage: 00 | Mode: Auto | Time: ~5 min

docs.title: What it does
docs.text: Creates the standard NSRR folder structure for a new dataset and initializes a README.md pre-filled with sections 0–13, matching the APPLES/NSRR harmonization standard.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
harmonix scaffold --dataset <name> --path <working_dir>

Example: 

harmonix scaffold --dataset apples --path /data/nsrr/working/apples

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Required Description --dataset Yes Dataset name (used in README title and file prefixes) --path Yes Working directory path to scaffold --readme-template No Path to custom README template (default: NSRR standard)

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
Creates the following folder structure:

<path>/
├── files/       # original non-data files (PDFs, XLS, metadata)
├── sl/          # Luna sample lists
├── tmp/         # raw Luna outputs (.db files)
├── res/         # compiled higher-level outputs
├── cmd/         # Luna scripts
├── nap/         # NAP output and harmonized EDFs/annotations
├── dist/        # final distribution folder
└── viz/         # visual summaries (auto-generated HTML)

And a README.md with all standard sections pre-populated:

0) Preliminaries
1) Key design factors
2) EDF checks
3) Identifiers
4) Signal review
5) Annotation review
6) Manual staging
7) NAP signal mappings
8) NAP annotation mappings
9) Encodings
10) Interim report
11) Running NAP
12) Compiling the distribution dataset
13) Depositing an as-is dataset

docs.title: README template
docs.text:
The README is initialized with dataset name, date, working paths, and placeholders for all sections. Subsequent HARMONIX tools populate these sections automatically as they run.

Tip

You should fill in Section 1 (Key design factors) manually after scaffolding — this is where you document study design, number of subjects, sites, PSG system used, and any known quirks of the dataset.

docs.title: Tutorials
docs.text: Tutorials walk through end-to-end use of HARMONIX on real PSG datasets.

docs.title: Available tutorials
docs.text:
Tutorial Dataset What you'll learn Your First Dataset APPLES (NSRR) Full pipeline, start to finish

More tutorials coming as the framework develops.

docs.title: Tutorial: Your First Dataset
docs.text: In this tutorial we walk through harmonizing the APPLES dataset using HARMONIX end-to-end. APPLES has 1,105 subjects across 5 sites, all using the Alice4 PSG system — a good representative real-world case.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• HARMONIX installed (pip install -e .)
  • Luna v1.2+ in your PATH
  • ERISTwo cluster access
  • APPLES EDFs at /data/nsrr/working/apples-edfs/

docs.title: Step 1 — Scaffold the project
docs.text:
harmonix scaffold --dataset apples --path /data/nsrr/working/apples
cd /data/nsrr/working/apples

Check what was created:

ls
# files/  sl/  tmp/  res/  cmd/  nap/  dist/  viz/  README.md

Open README.md and fill in Section 1 (Key design factors): - 1,105 individuals, one EDF each - 5 sites: SU, UA, SM, SL, BW - All studies: Alice4 PSG system - Each subject has a STAGE.csv file

docs.title: Step 2 — Build the sample list
docs.text:
luna --build ../apples-edfs/ > sl/s0.lst
# wrote 1105 EDFs to the sample list

docs.title: Step 3 — PHI validation
docs.text:
harmonix phi --sl sl/s0.lst

Expected output: 

PHI check: 1105 subjects scanned
PASS: 1103
SOFT [non-standard filename]: 2 subjects → review_queue.txt
README §3 updated.

docs.title: Step 4 — Generate cmap
docs.text:
harmonix cmap --sl sl/s0.lst --out cmd/cmap.txt

The tool will show you any low-confidence mappings and ask you to confirm:

? C3-M2 → C3 [confidence: 0.97] ✓ auto-accepted
? C4-M1 → C4 [confidence: 0.96] ✓ auto-accepted
? "EEG Cz-ref" → ?? [confidence: 0.61] — please confirm:
  Options: [1] CZ  [2] skip  [3] custom

docs.title: Step 5 — Run QC pipeline
docs.text:
Submit to SLURM:

harmonix qc --sl sl/s0.lst --cmap cmd/cmap.txt --jobs 20

This submits 20 array jobs. Monitor with:

squeue -u $USER

When complete:

harmonix qc --merge --out res/qc_summary.txt

docs.title: Step 6 — Review coverage dashboard
docs.text:
harmonix coverage --res res/ --out res/coverage_report.html

Open res/coverage_report.html in a browser. Review the inclusion/exclusion table and adjust thresholds if needed.

docs.title: Step 7 — Work through review queue
docs.text:
cat res/review_queue.txt

Open hard-flagged subjects in LunaScope first. Resolve or document each one. Add resolution notes to res/issues.md.

docs.title: Step 8 — Generate issues file
docs.text:
harmonix issues --res res/ --out res/issues.md

Add narrative context to any entries that need it.

docs.title: Step 9 — Finalize report
docs.text:
harmonix report --readme README.md --out dist/apples_harmonization_report.html

Your report is ready at dist/apples_harmonization_report.html.

Done

You've harmonized APPLES. The dist/ folder contains harmonized EDFs, the issues file, and the full harmonization report — ready for NSRR deposit.
